title: MCP-A query Response
description: Response for the query primitive (SPEC §3). Covers both prose mode and structured-response mode. `answer_id` and `citations` are always required; `structured`/`structured_schema_ref` are present only when `response_schema` was supplied.

answer.description: Compiled natural-language answer. MAY be omitted or null when structured output is returned and include_prose=false; a short summary when include_prose=true.

structured.description: Typed objects conforming to the requested response_schema, with server-side aggregations and disambiguation applied. Present only when response_schema was supplied.

structured_schema_ref.description: Which schema/ontology the structured payload conforms to. MUST be set whenever `structured` is returned.

answer_id.description: Opaque handle for follow_up and explain calls. MUST be present.

citations.description: Sources supporting the answer. MUST be present (also for structured payloads), each with at least source_system.

recommended_tool.description: Optional drill path to a specific domain/tool for deeper detail.

reason.description: Why this tool might provide more detail.

routing_decision.description: Routing detail, present only when debug/explain was requested (see explain primitive).

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the answer was compiled.

is_draft.description: OPTIONAL. True when options.draft was true and the answer is incomplete (poll via follow_up). Absent or false means a normal, complete answer; `is_draft: true` appears only on draft/partial answers (SPEC §3).

status.description: OPTIONAL outcome discriminator (SPEC §3, MAEP-0005). Absent ⇒ a normal completed answer. `clarification_required` = the server understood the intent but needs more specificity before it can compile a full answer; the `clarification` object then describes what it needs. `completed` = a full answer is present.

clarification.description: Present if and only if `status` is `clarification_required` (SPEC §3, MAEP-0005). Describes the information the server still needs, reusing the same `ClarificationField` shape as `action` (MAEP-0003). The client resolves it by re-calling `query` with `query_id` + `clarification_inputs`.

needed.description: The fields the client supplies (keyed by `name`) in the continuation request's `clarification_inputs` map.

prompt.description: Optional human-readable prompt summarizing what the client should provide.
